mira-intro.speaker: Mira
mira-intro.text: You are awake. Good. WASD moves you, Enter talks, and Escape opens the field menu.

mira-intro.speaker: Mira
mira-intro.text: Quest: A Leaf Still Burning. Gather three Emberleaf from the trail flowers.

mira-intro.speaker: Mira
mira-intro.text: After that, cross to the slime on the bridge and win one clean fight. Return to me when both are done.

mira-return.speaker: Mira
mira-return.text: Quest: gather three Emberleaf, then defeat the bridge slime. Your quest log tracks the next step.

mira-return:ready.speaker: Mira
mira-return:ready.text: You have the leaves and a real victory. Brindlemarket needs you now.

mira-return:ready.speaker: Mira
mira-return:ready.text: Quest complete. The east road is open; follow it to Brindlemarket.

mira-return:done.speaker: Mira
mira-return:done.text: Follow the road east. Brindlemarket is waiting.

waystone.speaker: Old Waystone
waystone.text: Moonroot Hollow opens only to those who bring light, nerve, and a key cut from old roots.

elder-bram.speaker: Elder Bram
elder-bram.text: The roots beneath our village are sick. Moonroot Hollow holds the bloom we need.

elder-bram.speaker: Elder Bram
elder-bram.text: Prepare at the market, then take the north-east gate.

pippa.speaker: Pippa
pippa.text: Pouches light? Shelves full. Take a look.

luma.speaker: Luma
luma.text: A warm bowl and a clean bandage fix more than pride.

nia-rumor.speaker: Nia
nia-rumor.text: A merchant is selling fake Moonroot charms. I saw the paint still wet.

nia-rumor.speaker: Nia
nia-rumor.text: After the dungeon, remind me. I know where a real lucky thread is hidden.

porter-moss.speaker: Porter Moss
porter-moss.text: Every crate here is paid for by someone brave enough to step into Moonroot Hollow and lucky enough to come back.

wounded-delver.speaker: Wounded Delver
wounded-delver.text: The healers say I should rest. The guild says my map is worth more than my sword arm. Both are probably right.

fountain-runner.speaker: Fountain Runner
fountain-runner.text: Toss a coin for the fountain, then run to the gate before the delvers take all the stew.

gate-guard.speaker: Gate Guard
gate-guard.text: Moonroot Hollow keeps this town fed, shod, and scared. Elder Bram decides who passes the north-east gate.

supply-clerk.speaker: Supply Clerk
supply-clerk.text: Rope, lantern oil, antidotes, clean socks. The dungeon taxes fools first and the unprepared second.

rumor-merchant.speaker: Rumor Merchant
rumor-merchant.text: Buy a charm, hear a rumor, ignore both if you value your purse. Nia has been watching me too closely.

echo-sprite.speaker: Echo Sprite
echo-sprite.text: The backroom wakes when the key is found and three lanterns remember the moon.

moon-gate.speaker: Moon Gate
moon-gate.text: The stone waits for a root key and three lit lanterns.

floor_1_quartermaster_intro.speaker: Quartermaster Sella
floor_1_quartermaster_intro.text: Forward base is open. Take the warm stones while you can; the north rooms are already arguing with us.

floor_1_mapper_intro.speaker: Guild Mapper
floor_1_mapper_intro.text: Chalk means someone came back. Missing chalk means either no one went there, or no one returned with hands steady enough to mark it.

floor_1_mapper_intro.speaker: Guild Mapper
floor_1_mapper_intro.text: Field lesson: follow the marked plates north. Let the safe spike plate teach your boots before the real traps do. Press Q if you need the Quest Log to repeat the objective.

floor_1_cooking_intro.speaker: Cook Marlo
floor_1_cooking_intro.text: Eat before the doors. Dungeon courage burns fast, and stew is cheaper than a rescue party.

floor_1_wounded_delver_warning.speaker: Wounded Delver
floor_1_wounded_delver_warning.text: The base is safe. Past the chalk fork, safe becomes a story people tell new delvers.

floor_1_rookie_training_hint.speaker: Guild Rookie
floor_1_rookie_training_hint.text: Practice plates are marked here. Deeper down, the Hollow lets you find the lesson with your boots. Press Q to check the Quest Log before you wander.

floor_1_descent_warning.speaker: Descent Guard
floor_1_descent_warning.text: Floor two is past guild control. Check your supplies, then step down like the stairs are listening.

floor_1_descent_warning.speaker: Descent Guard
floor_1_descent_warning.text: Bring me a reading from the root-cracked shrine before you take the stairs. Floor two does not grade gently.

floor_1_descent_warning:complete.speaker: Descent Guard
floor_1_descent_warning:complete.text: You've read the marks, sprung the traps, and seen what goblins leave behind. That is enough warning for floor two.

floor_1_shrine_lore.speaker: Root-Cracked Shrine
floor_1_shrine_lore.text: Moonroot light leaks through the stone. It feels less like a blessing than a wound kept open.

field_kitchen_intro.speaker: Cookpot
field_kitchen_intro.text: The cookfire is banked low, ready for whatever the party brings back from the Hollow.

training_dummy.speaker: Training Barricade
training_dummy.text: Fresh cuts on the wood mark where rookies learned to stop swinging and start looking.

moonroot-provisioner.speaker: Provisioner Sella
moonroot-provisioner.text: Floor one is where pride learns to read the stones before the stones read back.

moonroot-cook.speaker: Cook Marlo
moonroot-cook.text: Eat before descending. The Hollow does not pause for empty hands or empty bellies.

moonroot-smith.speaker: Smith Tovin
moonroot-smith.text: Past floor ten, the stone starts hitting back. Gear up while the lamps are still friendly.

floor-guard-a.speaker: Floor Guard
floor-guard-a.text: Floor two is live dungeon. The traps will not announce themselves like these training rooms.

floor-guard-b.speaker: Floor Guard
floor-guard-b.text: Boss floors lock the descent. Beat the warden on floor ten before floor eleven opens.

dungeon-checkpoints.speaker: Root Crystal
dungeon-checkpoints.text: Checkpoint crystals wait at floors 25, 50, and 75. Once reached, they become return anchors.

trap-pitfall.speaker: Pitfall Exhibit
trap-pitfall.text: Real pitfalls are rare. They drop delvers deeper by one to three floors early on, but never past an unbeaten boss floor.

trap-spikes.speaker: Spike Plate Exhibit
trap-spikes.text: A spike plate punishes careless steps. In the Hollow, the safest sign is often the one you almost missed.

trap-darts.speaker: Dart Line Exhibit
trap-darts.text: Dart lines fire from old wall seams. The leader usually catches the first lesson.

trap-poison.speaker: Poison Mist Exhibit
trap-poison.text: Poison mist vents pressure into a room. Antidotes are cheap until you need one.

trap-alarm.speaker: Alarm Rune Exhibit
trap-alarm.text: Alarm runes wake nearby creatures. If the floor goes quiet, check your footing.

trap-crumble.speaker: Crumbling Bridge Exhibit
trap-crumble.text: Crumbling bridges hold just long enough to make you trust them.

trap-teleport.speaker: Teleport Snare Exhibit
trap-teleport.text: Teleport snares do not kill. They separate plans from reality, which is sometimes worse.

goblin-grunt.speaker: Goblin Grunt
goblin-grunt.text: Two steps. Four directions. Goblin follows the guild rules.

moonroot-visitor.speaker: Moonroot Visitor
moonroot-visitor.text: Spliced in for a visual test. If the scale feels strange, blame the model mismatch.

mira-intro.speaker: Mira
talk: The guild likes its lessons neat, but the road never holds still long enough to be neat.
Emberleaf grows where old heat lingers. Pick it cleanly and the plant comes back stronger.
Brindlemarket sends more runners than it admits. Someone has to teach them which end of fear is useful.
My job is keeping new delvers alive until the guild can make them someone else's problem.
Life near Moonroot Hollow is all lists: supplies, warnings, names returned, names missing.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace Mira's trail rumour with final story copy later.

mira-return.speaker: Mira
talk: You learn faster than most, which means the road will start asking harder questions.
The guild calls this a field exercise. The Hollow calls everything practice until it hurts.
Keep your pack light enough to run and full enough to survive being wrong.
Brindlemarket has stew, gossip, and six versions of every warning. Listen to at least two.
I miss quiet mornings, but quiet mornings do not usually need a blade nearby.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace Mira's return rumour with final story copy later.

elder-bram.speaker: Elder Bram
talk: The guild guards the Hollow, but Brindlemarket pays the cost when it stirs.
A village built above hungry roots learns to count every harvest twice.
My job is choosing which bad news becomes an order and which waits until morning.
The old families remember when Moonroot light meant blessing. Now it means we check the locks.
If the guild seems cautious, good. Caution is the only tradition the Hollow has not swallowed.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace Elder Bram's village rumour with final story copy later.

pippa.speaker: Pippa
talk: Guild coin spends well, but guild rush orders always arrive five minutes before trouble.
I stock rope for clever people and extra rope for the ones who think they are clever.
Market life is mostly counting jars, smiling at panic, and pretending the shelves refill themselves.
The Hollow teaches brand loyalty. Survive once with my antidote and you come back for another.
If you hear glass clinking in my pack, that is profit trying not to break.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace Pippa's market rumour with final story copy later.

luma.speaker: Luma
talk: The guild underestimates stew because stew never writes reports.
A warm meal before the floor can turn a disaster into a story with a punchline.
My job is feeding people who swear they are not scared and charging them anyway.
Brindlemarket survives on Moonroot silver, garden onions, and everyone checking on everyone else.
If the benches look scratched, blame armor, ambition, and boots that forgot the mud outside.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace Luma's hearth rumour with final story copy later.

tovin.speaker: Tovin
talk: The guild wants gear light, cheap, and unbreakable. They may pick two if I like them.
A blade tells me more about a delver than their boast does.
My job is hammering fear into useful shapes: buckles, edge guards, hinge pins, sense.
Life in Brindlemarket rings like an anvil. Work, warning bell, work again.
Moonroot dust gets into scabbards. Clean your steel before the floor cleans it for you.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace Tovin's forge rumour with final story copy later.

nia-rumor.speaker: Nia
talk: The guild posts guards at gates and forgets rooftops have opinions.
I hear more from loose shingles than most people hear from witnesses.
My job is knowing who lied, who paid, and who thinks those are different skills.
Brindlemarket is small enough that every secret has errands to run.
Moonroot Hollow makes people nervous. Nervous people drop better clues.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace Nia's rooftop rumour with final story copy later.

porter-moss.speaker: Porter Moss
talk: The guild calls them requisitions. I call them boxes that bite my knees.
Every crate going into the Hollow weighs more after you read the destination tag.
My job is lifting supplies and pretending I do not count how many come back unused.
Brindlemarket's streets look peaceful because half of us are busy carrying the panic indoors.
If you need something moved fast, pay before the alarm bell starts.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace Porter Moss's supply rumour with final story copy later.

wounded-delver.speaker: Wounded Delver
talk: The guild says rest. My leg agrees, but my pride keeps filing objections.
Floor stone has a sound when it is about to betray you. I learned it one step late.
My job used to be scouting. For now it is being a warning with bandages.
Life after a bad run is soup, sleep, and remembering which friends made it out.
Do not mock chalk marks. Chalk marks are promises from people who paid for them.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace the wounded delver's warning rumour with final story copy later.

fountain-runner.speaker: Fountain Runner
talk: Guild messages move faster when someone small can dodge carts.
I know every shortcut between the fountain, the gate, and Luma's back door.
My job is running notes before the ink dries or the news gets worse.
Brindlemarket life is loudest at the square, which is why I listen there.
If you toss a coin in the fountain, wish for dry socks. Heroes forget practical magic.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace the fountain runner's square rumour with final story copy later.

gate-guard.speaker: Gate Guard
talk: The guild would rather delay ten heroes than bury one fool.
Gate duty is mostly saying no until someone important says it louder.
My job is watching the road, the Hollow, and the faces people make between them.
Brindlemarket sleeps better when this gate is boring. I treasure boring.
If Elder Bram has not cleared you, the north-east path stays a view.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace the gate guard's road rumour with final story copy later.

supply-clerk.speaker: Supply Clerk
talk: The guild can lose a sword and call it variance. Lose a ledger and everyone learns my name.
Floor kits go out clean and return muddy, dented, or not at all.
My job is making sure panic has a receipt and a spare lantern.
Brindlemarket runs on inventory. Food, bandages, nails, patience.
If you skip antidotes, the Hollow will notice and invoice you personally.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace the supply clerk's guild rumour with final story copy later.

rumor-merchant.speaker: Rumor Merchant
talk: The guild sells certainty. I sell the cheaper thing people actually buy.
A charm is only fake if the customer stops believing before leaving the stall.
My job is reading faces and pricing hope by the thread.
Brindlemarket calls me dishonest, then asks what I heard by supper.
Moonroot fear polishes coins better than any cloth.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace the rumor merchant's paid rumour with final story copy later.

echo-sprite.speaker: Echo Sprite
talk: Guild boots beat once. The floor answers twice.
Moonroot light remembers names better than stone remembers shape.
My job is echo, warning, mistake, maybe all three.
Life here is listening to doors dream about being walls.
The Hollow repeats what it likes and eats what it loves.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace the Echo Sprite's cryptic rumour with final story copy later.

floor_1_quartermaster_intro.speaker: Quartermaster Sella
talk: The guild base holds because every rope, ration, and lantern has a place.
Floor one is polite by dungeon standards, which is not the same as safe.
My job is telling brave people they packed like optimists.
Life down here smells like oilcloth, hot stone, and arguments over inventory.
If you borrow guild supplies, bring back either the item or a very convincing apology.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace Sella's quartermaster rumour with final story copy later.

floor_1_mapper_intro.speaker: Guild Mapper
talk: The guild trusts maps because maps at least admit where they stop knowing.
Floor one teaches markings, sightlines, and the humility to turn around.
My job is converting other people's near misses into useful chalk.
Life at the forward base is quiet until someone finds a new way to be surprised.
Read the floor before you read the room. The floor lies less often.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace the mapper's floor-one rumour with final story copy later.

floor_1_cooking_intro.speaker: Cook Marlo
talk: The guild measures morale in bowls whether the captains admit it or not.
Floor one burns through courage faster than lamp oil.
My job is keeping delvers fed enough to make better mistakes.
Life at the cookfire is steam, gossip, and checking who skips seconds.
A full belly will not save you from a trap, but it may help you notice one.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace Marlo's field-kitchen rumour with final story copy later.

floor_1_wounded_delver_warning.speaker: Wounded Delver
talk: The guild put my cot near the route so rookies can see what rushing buys.
Floor one is the handshake. Deeper floors are the grip tightening.
My current job is lying still and being educational.
Life in the infirmary is counting drips, footsteps, and how long pride takes to heal.
When the chalk changes color, slow down. Someone paid blood for that note.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace the floor-one wounded delver rumour with final story copy later.

floor_1_rookie_training_hint.speaker: Guild Rookie
talk: The guild says everyone starts somewhere. I keep wishing somewhere had fewer spikes.
Floor one looks smaller on the map and louder under your boots.
My job today is learning, not screaming, though the schedule allows overlap.
Life at base is veterans giving advice and then laughing when you write it down.
I practice watching the floor. The floor practices looking innocent.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace the rookie's training rumour with final story copy later.

floor_1_descent_warning.speaker: Descent Guard
talk: The guild stops calling it training at the stairs.
Floor one is supervised danger. Floor two is danger with opinions.
My job is making sure no one descends with empty packs or full arrogance.
Life at this post is listening to stairs and judging footsteps.
If you hesitate here, good. Hesitation means some part of you is still taking notes.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace the descent guard's stairs rumour with final story copy later.

moonroot-provisioner.speaker: Provisioner Sella
talk: The guild keeps caches on every stable floor and arguments beside most of them.
A lower floor changes what supplies mean. Rope becomes retreat, oil becomes time.
My job is turning lists into survival before the Hollow edits them.
Life between descents is sorting what came back from what needs replacing.
Pack for the floor you fear, not the floor you hope to find.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace Provisioner Sella's deep-floor rumour with final story copy later.

moonroot-cook.speaker: Cook Marlo
talk: The guild can plan routes all day. Hunger plans faster.
Dungeon floors make every meal taste like a checkpoint.
My job is feeding the brave, the lost, and the people pretending those are separate.
Life near the descent is a pot that never quite cools.
Eat while the lamps are friendly.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace Cook Marlo's deep-floor rumour with final story copy later.

moonroot-smith.speaker: Smith Tovin
talk: The guild sends me dents and asks for miracles by morning.
Past the early floors, armor stops being fashion and starts being evidence.
My job is arguing with metal until it agrees to keep someone alive.
Life beside the anvil is simple: heat, strike, quench, repeat, bill.
If your shield has new scratches, thank it before asking me to fix it.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace Smith Tovin's deep-floor rumour with final story copy later.

floor-guard-a.speaker: Floor Guard
talk: The guild posts us where confidence usually outruns memory.
Floor two stops explaining itself. Watch corners and quiet rooms.
My job is repeating warnings until someone survives long enough to repeat them back.
Life on guard is counting boots in and boots out.
The safest delver is the one who still thinks the floor can surprise them.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace Floor Guard A's patrol rumour with final story copy later.

floor-guard-b.speaker: Floor Guard
talk: The guild tracks boss floors like storms with stairs.
Every tenth floor changes the rules just enough to punish habits.
My job is keeping people from treating a locked descent like a suggestion.
Life here is paperwork, passwords, and waiting for the next alarm.
If the route forward closes, earn it open. That is the Hollow's language.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace Floor Guard B's boss-floor rumour with final story copy later.

dungeon-checkpoints.speaker: Root Crystal
talk: The guild marks crystals as anchors. The crystals do not ask what anchors are for.
Some floors remember arrivals more clearly than departures.
My job is waiting, shining, and pretending waiting is not a kind of hunger.
Life in crystal time is footsteps, silence, then footsteps again.
Touch the deep checkpoints carefully. Return paths notice who uses them.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace the checkpoint crystal rumour with final story copy later.

goblin-grunt.speaker: Goblin Grunt
talk: Guild boots loud. Goblin boots smart.
Floor has corners. Corners have goblins. Good floor.
My job is walking, watching, and not getting yelled at by bigger goblin.
Life in Hollow is shiny rocks, bad soup, and running when armor clanks.
Two steps one way, two steps back. Very official patrol.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace the goblin grunt rumour with final story copy later.

moonroot-visitor.speaker: Moonroot Visitor
talk: The guild said this floor was under control. I have questions about their definition.
Moonroot light makes every shadow look like it has been invited.
My job was observing. Now it is standing very still and hoping that counts.
Life as a visitor is mostly realizing the locals packed better than you did.
If the scale of this place feels wrong, trust that feeling.

lines: RUMOUR PLACEHOLDER: Replace the Moonroot Visitor's odd rumour with final story copy later.
